237	/// `tamarin-prover variants` — mirror HS's `Main.Mode.Intruder.run`.
238	/// HS dumps the DH-intruder rule variants (the `c_exp`, `c_inv`,
239	/// `c_mult`, `c_one`, etc. rules) then the BP-intruder variants, without
240	/// needing a `.spthy` file (Intruder.hs:44-53).
241	///
242	/// We mirror the DH half: spin up Maude with `dh_maude_sig()`, generate the
243	/// rules via [`tamarin_theory::intruder_rules::dh_intruder_rules`] with the
244	/// HS-hardcoded `False` flag, and pretty-print each rule in HS's
245	/// `rule (modulo AC) NAME:` shape.  The BP half is a known gap (see body).
246	fn run_variants(args: &Args) -> Result<i32, RunError> {
247	    let maude_path = args.maude_path.clone().unwrap_or_else(default_maude_path);
248	    // HS `Main.Mode.Intruder.run` (Intruder.hs:44-53) starts TWO SEPARATE
249	    // Maude handles — one on `dhMaudeSig`, one on `bpMaudeSig` — and
250	    // generates `dhIntruderRules False` then `bpIntruderRules False`, then
251	    // emits `dhS ++ bpS`.  We mirror the DH handle on `dh_maude_sig()` ALONE
252	    // (NOT merged with bp): merging exposes pmult/em to Maude during the DH
253	    // variant query and could perturb DH variant enumeration.  The DH
254	    // generator is hardcoded `False` in HS, not the --diff flag, so we pass
255	    // `false`.
256	    let sig = tamarin_term::maude_sig::dh_maude_sig();
257	    let maude = MaudeHandle::start(&maude_path, sig).map_err(|e| {
258	        RunError(format!("failed to start maude at {:?}: {:?}", maude_path, e))
259	    })?;
260	    // HS emits the maude tool/version banner on STDERR (via `ensureMaude`),
261	    // not stdout — the rule dump alone goes to stdout.  Mirror that.
262	    if let Some(v) = crate::cli::detect_maude_version_pub() {
263	        print_maude_banner(&maude_path, Some(&v));
264	    }
265	    // HS `Main.Mode.Intruder.run` (Intruder.hs:48-53) generates BOTH the DH
266	    // and the bilinear-pairing variants and emits `dhS ++ bpS`:
267	    //   - DH: `dhIntruderRules False` (runtime, via Maude).  RS's runtime
268	    //     generator is byte-faithful (exactly 51 rules); `variants_intruder`
269	    //     applies `remove_renamings` to drop redundant identity-variants.
270	    //   - BP: `bpIntruderRules False` (runtime).  Like HS
271	    //     (Intruder.hs:50), we start a SECOND Maude handle on
272	    //     `bp_maude_sig()` and generate the 75 BP rules at runtime via
273	    //     `bp_intruder_rules(false, ..)`.  This tracks the CURRENT Maude
274	    //     rather than the stale cached `data/intruder_variants_bp.spthy`
275	    //     (which production proving still parses via
276	    //     `mk_bp_intruder_variants`); HS's `variants` command likewise
277	    //     generates BP at runtime, so the two stay byte-identical.
278	    let dh_rules = tamarin_theory::intruder_rules::dh_intruder_rules(false, &maude);
279	    let bp_sig = tamarin_term::maude_sig::bp_maude_sig();
280	    let bp_maude = MaudeHandle::start(&maude_path, bp_sig).map_err(|e| {
281	        RunError(format!("failed to start maude at {:?}: {:?}", maude_path, e))
282	    })?;
283	    let bp_rules = tamarin_theory::intruder_rules::bp_intruder_rules(false, &bp_maude);
284	    // HS `putStrLn (dhS ++ bpS)` where each block is
285	    // `renderDoc . prettyIntruderVariants` (Rule.hs:1343): blank-line-separated
286	    // `rule (modulo AC) NAME:` rules with HughesPJ body wrapping (`sep`/`fsep`
287	    // at the standard width) and NO trailing newline — so the DH and BP blocks
288	    // abut (the DH `d_inv` body directly precedes the BP `c_pmult` header with
289	    // no separating newline).  `putStrLn` appends the single trailing newline.
290	    let dh_s = tamarin_theory::pretty_formula::pretty_intruder_variants(&dh_rules);
291	    let bp_s = tamarin_theory::pretty_formula::pretty_intruder_variants(&bp_rules);
292	    print!("{}{}", dh_s, bp_s);
293	    println!();
294	    Ok(0)
295	}

300	/// Run the interactive web UI. Mirrors `Main.Mode.Interactive.run`:
301	/// builds a [`tamarin_server::ServerConfig`] from the CLI flags, eagerly
302	/// loads any positional `.spthy` files into the theory store, and serves
303	/// HTTP until SIGINT/SIGTERM. Returns 0 on graceful shutdown.
304	fn run_interactive(args: &Args) -> Result<i32, RunError> {
305	    use std::net::{IpAddr, Ipv4Addr, SocketAddr};
306	
307	    init_rayon_pool(args);
308	
309	    // Haskell defaults: 3001 on 127.0.0.1.
310	    let port = args.port.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_INTERACTIVE_PORT);
311	
312	    // `--interface` accepts a literal IP address. Haskell's `*4` / `*` /
313	    // `*6` magic strings bind to all interfaces; mirror those.
314	    let iface_str = args
315	        .interface
316	        .clone()
317	        .unwrap_or_else(|| "127.0.0.1".to_string());
318	    let ip: IpAddr = match iface_str.as_str() {
319	        "*" | "*4" => IpAddr::V4(Ipv4Addr::UNSPECIFIED),
320	        "*6" => IpAddr::V6(std::net::Ipv6Addr::UNSPECIFIED),
321	        other => other.parse::<IpAddr>().map_err(|e| {
322	            RunError(format!(
323	                "could not parse --interface={:?} as an IP address: {}\n\
324	                 Use --interface=\"*4\" to bind to all IPv4 interfaces.",
325	                other, e,
326	            ))
327	        })?,
328	    };
329	    let bind_addr = SocketAddr::new(ip, port);

632	- It carries NO accompanying warning or advisory: the YellowTest `--prove`
633	  summary has no WARNING section and no `The analysis results might be wrong!`
634	  line, and stderr is the plain 5 phases + `[Saturating Sources] Done`
635	  `[r7_yellow_prove.err]`. So this state does not feed the might-be-wrong advisory
636	  of §12b (that advisory tracks a wellformedness-check failure under `--prove`,
637	  not proof incompleteness).
638	- It is a PROVEN-terminal state: it appears only when the lemma is actually
639	  proved. A DEFAULT (no `--prove`) run of the same theory reports all four lemmas
640	  as `analysis incomplete (1 steps)` `[r7_yellow_default]` — the phrase requires
641	  the proof to run and hit the wall.
642	
643	### 15b. Bound exhaustion is DISTINCT (still `analysis incomplete`)
644	`--prove --bound=2` on the SAME theory cuts every proof short BEFORE it reaches
645	the `UNFINISHABLE` step, so all four lemmas read `analysis incomplete (4 steps)`
646	`[r7_yellow_bound]` — never the reducible-operators phrase. This directly answers
647	the Round-7 question: a bounded/unfinished-by-bound analysis and a
648	reducible-operators wall are DIFFERENT verdicts even for the identical lemmas
649	(the bound one reuses the existing `analysis incomplete`, §12c). `--stop-on-trace=
650	SORRY` does NOT change the phrasing: it still shows the reducible-operators phrase
651	for the wall lemmas and verified/falsified for the rest `[r7_yellow_sotsorry]`
652	(consistent with §14a `r6_sot_*`).
653	
654	### 15c. It composes with the `--diff` side prefixes and both kinds
655	`--diff --prove` on `csf23-subterms/YellowDiffTest.spthy` (whose `diff(...)`
656	makes only the LHS projection reducible) yields `[r7_yellowdiff_prove]`:
657	
658	    RHS :  GreenYellow (exists-trace): verified (3 steps)
659	    LHS :  GreenYellow (exists-trace): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
660	    RHS :  RedYellow (all-traces): falsified - found trace (3 steps)
661	    LHS :  RedYellow (all-traces): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
662	    RHS :  YellowRed (exists-trace): falsified - no trace found (3 steps)
663	    LHS :  YellowRed (exists-trace): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
664	    RHS :  YellowGreen (all-traces): verified (3 steps)
665	    LHS :  YellowGreen (all-traces): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
666	    DiffLemma:  Observational_equivalence : falsified - found trace (9 steps)
667	
668	So the phrase is rendered by the SAME shared verdict path (§14f `verdict_phrase`)
669	as a whole-theory lemma: it slots behind the `RHS :  `/`LHS :  ` prefix
670	unchanged, for both trace-kinds, exactly like every other verdict; the sides are
671	independent (RHS closes, LHS hits the wall).
672	
673	### 15d. Other candidate states re-confirmed to add NO new phrase
674	- `[sources]`/typing lemmas: with `--auto-sources`, the auto-generated
675	  `AUTO_typing (all-traces): verified (12 steps)` renders as a plain lemma line —
676	  no special "sources" summary line `[r7_openchains_autosrc]`. A theory whose
677	  partial deconstructions never resolve just proof-searches forever under a plain
678	  `--prove` (killed by timeout, exit 124, empty stdout — a resource condition, not
679	  a summary phrase) `[r7_openchains_prove]`. Confirms §14a: partial
680	  deconstructions / open chains add no summary-of-summaries line.
681	- Induction: a lemma marked `[use_induction]` (exists-trace or all-traces) proves
682	  normally to `verified`/`falsified` with no distinct phrase and no error
683	  `[r7_induction_prove]`.
684	- Lemma errors / solver failures / `--prove=<pattern>` non-match: unchanged from
685	  §14e / §12b — they abort with a runtime stderr line or feed the WARNING
686	  count-line slot, never a per-lemma verdict.
687	
688	### 15e. Clean-crate model
689	`framing::LemmaResult` gains a fourth variant `AnalysisCannotBeFinished`;
690	`verdict_phrase` maps it to the canned
691	`analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms)` for both kinds, so
692	`Whole`/`Rhs`/`Lhs`/`Diff` rendering picks it up automatically (no per-side code).

in the clean `LemmaResult`, the clean framing can express every batch-reachable
verdict, so routing the driver's summary through it is byte-neutral rather than a
silent regression. DELETED from `run.rs` (headered file, deletion of ported
expression is allowed):

* `print_overall_summary`'s hand-rolled block assembly — the `=`x78 rule,
  `analyzed:`/`output:` column alignment, the `WARNING:` count/advisory block, the
  `$--$` blank-line-gap logic, and the per-theory blank-line joins. It is REPLACED
  by a call to the clean `framing::render_summary(&summaries)` over a
  `Vec<framing::Summary>` mapped from the `FileResult`s (`to_clean_summary` /
  `to_clean_outcome` adapters).
* `format_lemma_summary_line` — the ported per-lemma verdict-phrase strings
  (`verified` / `falsified - found trace` / `falsified - no trace found` /
  `analysis incomplete` / `analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in
  subterms)`). Those phrases now live ONLY in the clean `framing::verdict_phrase`;
  `run.rs` keeps only a `map_verdict` classifier (`LemmaVerdict` ->
  `framing::LemmaResult`), no phrase text.
* The `[Theory <name>] <phase>` progress-marker string assembly — the `marker`
  closure now emits `framing::progress_line(&name, framing::Phase::…)` (all six call
  sites map to a clean `Phase` variant), deleting the RS `"[Theory {}] {}"` format.

Streaming contract: the `[Saturating Sources] …` lines are emitted deep inside
`tamarin-theory::prove` during proving (not through the driver), so a full
`emit::BatchEmitter` restructure of `run.rs` is infeasible without threading a
`Sink` through the theory crate. The driver already streams progress/payload as
produced; the only trailing block is the summary. `framing::render_summary` produces
byte-for-byte the same stdout the incremental `emit::BatchEmitter::finish` would
flush, and `framing::progress_line` is exactly `BatchEmitter::progress`'s per-marker
output — so the driver emits the emitter's per-stream bytes with the consumer
(`println!`/`eprint!`) controlling flush timing, satisfying step 2d for the
driver-reachable surface. The `--diff` projections (`RHS`/`LHS`/`DiffLemma`) are
present + pinned in the clean framing but UNREACHABLE through RS's driver
(`run_batch` still errors "`--diff` … is not yet ported"), exactly as round-7 found;
they are exercised only by the ten `console_split_parity` diff tests.

Retired pin (task-sanctioned): the run.rs inline
`lemma_summary_distinguishes_undetermined_and_invalidated` (+ its `mk_result`
helper) asserted the RS-only render strings `analysis undetermined` /
`proof has been invalidated` for `UndeterminedProof`/`InvalidatedProof`. The console
cluster's round-7 systematic probe (BEHAVIOR.md §15d) established that NEITHER state
is reachable from any batch run of the reference — no such phrase surfaced anywhere
in the example corpus; lemma errors/solver failures abort with a runtime stderr line
and never a per-lemma verdict. So the pin fixed invented behavior, not reference
parity; retiring it does not weaken any byte assertion against the reference. The
three batch-reachable verdicts it also touched are pinned byte-for-byte against
oracle captures by the `console_split_parity` framing suite. `map_verdict` collapses
the two batch-unreachable verdicts (and the RS-internal `Error`, which still
escalates the exit code) to `AnalysisIncomplete` so the summary stays well-formed
without authoring a non-reference phrase. (prover lib 61 -> 60.)

## D.3 Parse / value-validation / error-stream swap (blockers 1-3) — KEPT; restructure named

Kept ported (headers intact): `cli/mod.rs` `parse_args`/`Args`/validation +
tokenizer helpers, `main.rs` error contract. The clean modules already REPRODUCE the
faithful contract (proven by `console_split_parity`'s `error_streams_are_assigned` /
`integer_flag_errors_match_reference` / `no_input_files_envelope_includes_global_help`);
wiring them is a run-driver restructure, not a drop-in. Fresh reference probing of
the 1.13.0 binary pins the exact target contract and shows the CURRENT kept RS path
diverges on every point:

| input | reference (1.13.0) | current kept RS |
|-------|--------------------|-----------------|
| `--bound=x FILE` | preamble(stderr) THEN `tamarin-prover: bound: invalid bound given`+CallStack(stderr) | `error: bound: expected integer, got "x"`+help, ALL stderr, NO preamble |
| `--nonsense` | bare `Unknown flag: --nonsense`(stderr) | `error: unknown flag: --nonsense`+help(stderr) |
| `--prove` (no file) | `error: no input files given\n\n<help>` on STDOUT | same text+help on STDERR |

New this round: **the clean parse model is strictly MORE faithful than the ported
tokenizer** (a positive-value finding, not a wash) — reference probing confirms
`int --help` prefix-matches the interactive mode (clean `Mode::prefix_matches`; ported
requires exact `interactive`), `test-prover` is treated as a FILE not the test mode
(ported has an RS-only `"test-prover"` alias), and `-vh` is `Unknown flag: -h` (ported
accepts GNU-style clustering as verbose+help — an RS-ism). So the deletion is a
faithfulness improvement, which RAISES the verification bar rather than lowering it.

Why KEPT (the coupled restructure, precisely):
1. **Blocker 1 (validation ordering) is structural.** The reference emits the maude
   preamble THEN the value-validation error; the preamble is printed *inside*
   `run_batch`. Faithful routing means calling clean `parse::parse` early (structural
   + positional-arity/no-input, stream-routed) and DEFERRING clean `args::build_args`
   (the 8-flag value validation) into `run_batch` AFTER the preamble — which requires
   threading an unvalidated `RunSpec` (mode + positional + raw `Options`) through
   `run()` instead of the current `run(&Args)`. Any eager path (validate in
   `parse_args`/`main`) errors before the preamble and reproduces the divergence
   above. There is no drop-in; keeping `run(&Args)` and eager validation cannot
   satisfy blocker 1.
2. **Blocker 2 (error streams) rewrites `main.rs`'s single contract.** Bare
   cmdargs one-liners must go to stderr and the `error: … + full help` envelopes to
   STDOUT via the clean `errors` module — replacing `main.rs`'s uniform
   `error: <msg>\n\n<help> -> stderr`. It lands together with blocker 1 (the no-input
   envelope must move from a `run_batch` `RunError` to the parse phase's stdout
   route).
3. **~20 headered `cli/mod.rs` inline tests pin no-file argv** (e.g.
   `parse(&["-b12"])`, `parse(&["--quiet","--verbose"])`, `parse(&[])`), which the
   clean `parse` rejects at parse time as `no input files given` (a faithful
   positional-arity check the ported tokenizer omits). Routing `parse_args` through
   the clean pipeline breaks all of them; each needs a dummy input FILE added, and
   the RS-ism pins (`clustered_boolean_shorts`, `clustered_bool_then_value_short`,
   the `test-prover`/exact-subcommand assumptions, the `bound_bare_vs_absent` Some(5)
   vs clean None) need retiring/adjusting against the reference.
4. **It does not reduce the header count** — `cli/mod.rs` stays headered (it keeps
   the `Args`/`Subcommand`/`effective_processors`/`lemma_matches` surface), so the
   deletion is line-level cleanup gated on the full parse/validation/error byte-parity
   gate. Landing it half-validated is exactly the silent-regression risk the protocol
   forbids; the Rust-only `--processors`/`--maude-processes`/`--data-dir` flags (which
   the clean typed-args model already carries as `INTEROP_FLAGS`) must be routed and
   re-verified in the same pass.

Recommended next wave (single, dedicated): change `run()` to take the clean
`RunSpec`, call `build_args` post-preamble in `run_batch`, route errors through the
clean `errors` module by `Stream`, map clean `Args` -> ported `Args` (blocker-3
stop-on-trace recovered via `Options::is_set`), delete the ported tokenizer/validator
bodies + helpers, re-baseline the ~20 inline tests + `cli_e2e`'s
`no_input_files_returns_error` against the reference, and gate on the full example
corpus + the `--bound=x` / unknown-flag / no-input spot-check triplet.
